name: Montañas Vacías and Alto Tajo Loop
name_original: Parque Natural del Alto Tajo – circular por el río Cabrillas y Tajo hasta el salto de Poveda
description: This moderate 17.7-mile mountain bike loop in Alto Tajo Natural Park explores river canyons, pine forests, and the Salto de Poveda waterfall
region_intro: Mountain biking around Taravilla offers diverse terrain within the captivating landscapes of Spain. The region is characterized by the dramatic Tagus River Canyon, featuring impressive canyons, ravines, forests, and mountains. A notable natural feature is the Laguna de Taravilla, a unique lagoon within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. This area provides varied and engaging terrain for mountain bikers, including sections suitable for bikes along the Tagus River. Best mountain bike trails around Taravilla The most popular mountain bike trail is Natural Park of the Upper Tajo – circular from Peralejos de las Truchas to Laguna Taravilla, a 25.0 miles (40.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route connects Peralejos de las Truchas to Laguna Taravilla within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montañas Vacías and Alto Tajo Loop, a moderate 17.7 miles (28.5 km) path. This loop offers varied terrain, connecting the Montañas Vacías area with the Alto Tajo. Local mountain bikers also love the Laguna de Taravilla – Trail along the Tajo River loop from Peñalén, a 45.2 miles (72.7 km) trail leading through the Tajo River valley, often completed in about 6 hours 29 minutes. Mountain biking around Taravilla is defined by the Tagus River Canyon, Laguna de Taravilla, and the diverse terrains of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Taravilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Taravilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Taravilla?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails around Taravilla listed on komoot, offering diverse terrain within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. These routes cater to both moderate and difficult skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Taravilla?
A: Mountain biking in Taravilla features a variety of terrain, including impressive canyons, ravines, forests, and mountains, particularly within the dramatic Tagus River Canyon. You'll find varied and engaging paths, from riverside trails to climbs with rewarding views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Taravilla?
A: Yes, Taravilla offers several excellent circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004755251/natural-park-of-the-upper-tajo-circular-from-peralejos-de-las-truchas-to-laguna-taravilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Upper Tajo – circular from Peralejos de las Truchas to Laguna Taravilla&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 40 km trail. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004722315/montanas-vacias-and-alto-tajo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías and Alto Tajo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain over 28.5 km.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while mountain biking in Taravilla?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6498158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Taravilla&lt;/a&gt;, a lagoon formed by a karst spring. The dramatic Tagus River Canyon offers stunning views, and you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Poveda Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6268434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho del Horcajo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Towers of Chequilla&lt;/a&gt; are also a distinctive sight.

Q: Are there any difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Taravilla offers several challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14887130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Taravilla – Trail along the Tajo River loop from Peñalén&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 72.7 km trail with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River – Puente de Peñalén loop from Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 114 km.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Taravilla?
A: The mountain bike trails in Taravilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 10 reviews. Riders often praise the stunning natural environment, the dramatic Tagus River Canyon, and the varied terrain within the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: Is it possible to mountain bike near the Laguna de Taravilla?
A: Yes, the area around the Laguna de Taravilla is a prime attraction for mountain biking, known for its incredible trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004755251/natural-park-of-the-upper-tajo-circular-from-peralejos-de-las-truchas-to-laguna-taravilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Upper Tajo – circular from Peralejos de las Truchas to Laguna Taravilla&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that includes this beautiful lagoon.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Tagus River?
A: Many trails in Taravilla follow or offer views of the Tagus River, which carves impressive canyons and ravines through the landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14887130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Taravilla – Trail along the Tajo River loop from Peñalén&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads directly through the Tajo River valley, providing extensive riverside riding.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Taravilla?
A: While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, the region's diverse terrain and natural features suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations within the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for families in Taravilla?
A: While the guide primarily lists moderate to difficult trails, the varied terrain of the Alto Tajo Natural Park often includes sections that are less demanding. For family-friendly options, look for shorter, less steep sections of trails or consider exploring the flatter areas around the Laguna de Taravilla, though specific 'easy' rated routes are not highlighted in this guide.

Q: Do I need a permit to mountain bike in the Alto Tajo Natural Park?
A: The Alto Tajo Natural Park encompasses many of the trails around Taravilla. While general access is often permitted, it's always advisable to check the official regulations of the Alto Tajo Natural Park for any specific permit requirements for mountain biking, especially for certain protected areas or during particular seasons.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in Taravilla?
A: Parking availability will vary depending on the specific trailhead you choose. Many routes, especially those starting from villages like Peralejos de las Truchas or Peñalén, will likely have designated parking areas. It's recommended to check the starting point details of individual komoot tours for specific parking information.
tips: Bridge built in 1943 in stone masonry formed by two large arches that connect the two banks of the Tagus River.
Route of the Salto de Poveda and the Taravilla Lagoon, passing through the suspension bridge. One of the most emblematic routes of the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

What an exclusive paradise and privileged setting this lagoon has, whose waters come almost exclusively from underground seepage. Swimming is permitted in this lagoon, but recreational diving is prohibited.
Located on the Tagus River, next to the Taravilla lagoon, it is an idyllic place to take a bath from which you can contemplate views of the Tagus Valley.

They are a series of rural houses called "El Salto" (The Salto) located in a privileged setting, very close to the Tajo suspension bridge, and are accessed from the road via a dirt forest track. This place also has a small restaurant where you can have lunch and spend some pleasant moments.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It requires good physical fitness and some prior mountain biking experience. You will encounter diverse terrain, including unpaved tracks and natural trails, some with considerable slopes. While not for absolute beginners, riders with a reasonable fitness level and basic off-road skills should find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a true mountain biking experience. You can expect a mix of unpaved tracks, asphalt sections, gravel paths, and natural trails. Some parts may feature narrow paths (senderos estrechos) and wider tracks (pistas), with certain segments having notable inclines. The sections leading to the Salto de Poveda Waterfall are generally well-maintained.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural beauty and notable landmarks. Key highlights include the spectacular Salto de Poveda Waterfall, the serene Laguna de Taravilla, and a picturesque Suspension bridge over the River Tajo. You'll also pass by the historic Puente de Peñalén. The entire journey offers continuous and impressive views of the Alto Tajo Natural Park's gorges, canyons, and pine forests.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during spring or autumn. These seasons offer more pleasant temperatures and stable weather conditions, ideal for mountain biking. Summer can be very hot, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potential snow or ice, making some sections challenging or inaccessible.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, given the route's name and highlights, it's highly probable that you can find parking facilities near Poveda de la Sierra or close to the Salto de Poveda Waterfall. Look for designated parking spots in these areas, as they are common starting points for routes in the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for mountain biking in Alto Tajo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no entrance fee to access the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo. For mountain biking, specific permits are usually not required for established trails. However, it's always advisable to check the official park regulations before your visit, especially for any temporary restrictions or specific rules regarding certain areas.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo – circular por el río Cabrillas y Tajo hasta el salto de Poveda trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Alto Tajo Natural Park is generally welcoming to dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash at all times. This is for their safety, the protection of local wildlife, and to respect other trail users. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Natural del Tajo and the Montañas Vacías route for significant portions. It also briefly intersects with the Ruta por el Valle de las Cabrillas, the [CIMA GU06] Taravilla, and the [CIMA GU09] Almeriques.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restaurants or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by Casas del Salto Rural Accommodation and Restaurant, which can be a convenient stop for refreshments or a meal. Additionally, the nearby villages, such as Poveda de la Sierra, often offer further options for dining and lodging.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and natural setting, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube), and appropriate clothing for the weather. Sunscreen, a helmet, and a first-aid kit are also essential. Be aware that mobile phone coverage can be limited in some remote parts of the park.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect magnificent and high-value landscapes as you ride through the heart of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The route follows the Cabrillas and Tajo rivers, winding through impressive gorges and canyons, and passing through beautiful pine forests. The views are continuous and spectacular throughout the entire duration of the ride.
